key_insights.insight: Body language significantly affects first impressions, influencing how others perceive us.
key_insights.quote: 50% of our first impression is based around body language.

key_insights.insight: Body language isn't just about outward communication; it also sends signals back to our own brain, impacting our feelings of safety, confidence, and threat.
key_insights.quote: Body language isn't just us speaking to others. We're also speaking to ourselves. The way that we carry ourselves is sending messages back to our brain about whether we're safe or unsafe.

key_insights.insight: Forcing a smile, even if it's fake, can improve mood and make you feel happier because it causes the contraction of the muscles around the eyes.
key_insights.quote: The people who were in this forced smile…they were in a better mood. Their mood lifted.

key_insights.insight: People feeling powerful are more likely to view challenges as opportunities and be more creative.
key_insights.quote: When we feel powerful, we are more likely to take action, not just on behalf of ourselves, but also on behalf of others.

key_insights.insight: Self-affirmation involves reflecting on personal values and qualities, anchoring you to who you are, therefore making challenges less important.
key_insights.quote: Self-affirmation is…list the top two or three values or qualities that make them who they are…That's the exercise. They then have them do difficult things…they perform better.

key_insights.insight: When under pressure, avoid penguin arms (pinning wrists to hips and hands sticking out), as it signals low power and anxiety.
key_insights.quote: Penguin arms…is really common for people who are afraid of public speaking…they're afraid to move their arms away from their body.

key_insights.insight: Authenticity in communication is key, and inconsistencies between words and body language are easily perceived.
key_insights.quote: What matters is the asynchrony between the emotions conveyed with the words and the emotions conveyed with the body language.

key_insights.insight: Targets of bullying often have lower status in their profession but higher status outside their immediate in-group, making them vulnerable to resentment.
key_insights.quote: They have lower status in their profession than they do with the rest of the world... But the targets tend to be people who are below the average on status with the in-group and then cross what some researchers have called the line of resentment.

key_insights.insight: To effectively influence others, establishing trust is more important than demonstrating competence.
key_insights.quote: If you do not establish, earn trust, build trust, you have no medium through which your ideas can travel. So trust is the conduit of influence.

actionable_advice.advice: When feeling anxious or powerless, adopt expansive yoga poses or breathing exercises.
actionable_advice.how_to: Practice expansive yoga poses and slow, deep breathing to restore a sense of personal power and agency.

actionable_advice.advice: Before a stressful situation, find a private space and strike a power pose for a few minutes.
actionable_advice.how_to: Stand with hands on hips, or in a victory pose with arms up, to change the way you feel.

actionable_advice.advice: Audit your posture to identify signs of powerlessness, like pulled-in limbs and collapsed chest.
actionable_advice.how_to: Pay attention to your default seated position and how you carry yourself physically. Are you taking up a little space or are you being expansive.

actionable_advice.advice: Wake up in a starfish pose, or Usain Bolt pose, rather than curled in the fetal position.
actionable_advice.how_to: Stretch out when you wake up before putting your feet on the ground to reduce anxiety.

actionable_advice.advice: During team meetings, focus on changing one thing about your presentation to become better.
actionable_advice.how_to: Change the breathing to be more slow and deep. Be sure not to wrap your hands around your body by holding a water bottle, pen, or slide advancer.

actionable_advice.advice: Show openness with body language when speaking to people.
actionable_advice.how_to: Lean forward a little, keep palms open, show you are interested in them.

memorable_stories: The guest describes enduring years of academic bullying, where false information was circulated about her, and how it almost lead to her death.
The student who transformed herself from silent in the class to taking action, and now loves what she does.
A championship boxer who always finished his workout, even if he got a cramp, to continue building his mental narrative of who he is when things get tough.
contrarian_claims: The idea that eye contact is the most important cue of someone being deceptive.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Body Language Expert: 4 Body Language Tricks That Will Make Them Love You And Respect You! - Doctor Amy Cuddy
